title: Direct & Indirect Speech
definition: Direct speech reports the exact words a person said, in quotation marks. Indirect (reported) speech reports the meaning without quoting exact words, usually shifting the tense back one step.
formula: Direct: He said, "I am tired."  →  Indirect: He said (that) he was tired.
rules: Present Simple → Past Simple: "I eat rice" → He said he ate rice.
Present Continuous → Past Continuous: "I am working" → She said she was working.
Present Perfect → Past Perfect: "I have finished" → He said he had finished.
Past Simple → Past Perfect: "I went there" → She said she had gone there.
will → would, can → could, may → might, must → had to.
Pronouns and time/place words change: I→he/she, my→his/her, now→then, today→that day, here→there, tomorrow→the next day.
Questions in reported speech use if/whether (for yes/no) or the WH word, and normal statement word order (no auxiliary inversion): "Do you like tea?" → He asked if I liked tea.
examples: Direct: She said, "I am happy." → Indirect: She said she was happy.
Direct: He said, "I will call you." → Indirect: He said he would call me.
Direct: They said, "We have finished." → Indirect: They said they had finished.
Direct: She asked, "Do you like tea?" → Indirect: She asked if I liked tea.
Direct: He asked, "Where do you live?" → Indirect: He asked where I lived.
Direct: "I can swim," she said. → Indirect: She said she could swim.

commonMistakes.wrong: He said that he is tired.
commonMistakes.right: He said that he was tired.
commonMistakes.why: In reported speech, the tense usually shifts one step back (is → was).

commonMistakes.wrong: She asked did I like tea.
commonMistakes.right: She asked if I liked tea.
commonMistakes.why: Reported questions use normal word order with 'if/whether', not the inverted question form.

commonMistakes.wrong: He said he will come tomorrow.
commonMistakes.right: He said he would come the next day.
commonMistakes.why: 'Will' changes to 'would', and 'tomorrow' changes to 'the next day' in reported speech.

quiz.question: Convert: He said, "I am busy."
quiz.explanation: Present Simple 'am' shifts back to past 'was'.

quiz.question: Convert: She asked, "Where do you live?"
options: She asked where do I live.
She asked where did I live.
quiz.explanation: Reported WH-questions use normal word order and past tense.

quiz.question: Direct: "I will help you," he said. Indirect: He said he ___ help me.

quiz.sentence: He said that he will come.
quiz.correction: He said that he would come.
quiz.explanation: 'Will' shifts to 'would' when reporting past speech.
